1. Department Overview, Vision and Mission
Chunk ID: msajce_sh_chunk_01 | URL: https://www.msajce-edu.in/sh.php#tab1 | ~370 tokens
Overview of the S&H; department established in 2000, its role in engineering foundation, vision, mission and noted activities.
Department of Science and Humanities (S&H;)  MSAJCE
The department of S&H; was started in the year 2000. This department lays the base for furthering into one's engineering
career. Well experienced faculty with four doctorates contribute much in developing and moulding the students in enhancing
their knowledge to comprehend the basic subjects for better exposure. Students are guided through with innovative teaching
methods to make learning enjoyable. The laboratories available are well equipped to put into practice what the students have
learnt in theory classes. In addition to Literary and Debating Club activity, students are also exposed to various activities like
NSS, YRC, Physical Education and Yoga. The department holds the responsibility in making student-teacher and parent-teacher
interaction possible.
Vision:
To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient engineers through sustainable problems.
Mission:
(cid:127) M1. Impart fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Science & Humanities through innovative teaching and learning
methodology
(cid:127) M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for Industry and Society
(cid:127) M3. Cultivate the students with all-round competencies, for career, higher education and self-employability
Key Links:
(cid:127) Online Application: https://enrollonline.co.in/Registration/Apply/MSAJCE
(cid:127) 2021 Regulation Syllabus: https://www.msajce-edu.in/uploads/academics/2021Regulation.pdf

2. PEOs, PSOs and Programme Outcomes (POs)
Chunk ID: msajce_sh_chunk_02 | ~450 tokens
Programme Educational Objectives, Programme Specific Outcomes and all 12 Programme Outcomes for the S&H; department
(referenced under B.E. Mechanical Engineering).
PEOs and PSOs
Code Outcome
PEO1 Designing mechanical components and systems with creativity and sustainability
PEO2 Use modern tools for critical problem solving and industrial/societal analysis

Code Outcome
PEO3 Managerial and leadership skills for career and entrepreneurship
PSO1 Apply mechanical engineering knowledge to solve industrial problems using modern tools
PSO2 Develop cost-effective and eco-friendly mechanical components and systems
Programme Outcomes (POs):
(cid:127) PO1. Engineering knowledge: Apply mathematics, science and engineering fundamentals to complex problems
(cid:127) PO2. Problem analysis: Identify, formulate and analyse complex engineering problems using first principles
(cid:127) PO3. Design/development of solutions: Design solutions meeting specified needs with consideration for health, safety and
environment
(cid:127) PO4. Conduct investigation of complex problem: Use research-based knowledge, design of experiments and data analysis
(cid:127) PO5. Modern tool usage: Apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering/IT tools
(cid:127) PO6. The engineer and society: Assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues
(cid:127) PO7. Environment and sustainability: Understand impact of engineering solutions and need for sustainable development
(cid:127) PO8. Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics
(cid:127) PO9. Individual and team work: Function effectively as individual and in diverse multidisciplinary teams
(cid:127) PO10. Communication: Communicate effectively through reports, documentation and presentations
(cid:127) PO11. Project Management and Finance: Apply engineering and management principles to manage projects
(cid:127) PO12. Life-long learning: Engage in independent and life-long learning in the context of technological change
(cid:127) What are the PEOs for S&H; department at MSAJCE?

Keywords: PEO, PSO, PO, programme outcomes, OBE, outcomes based education, sustainability, ethics, teamwork, lifelong learning
Carry-forward: S&H; department started in 2000. Vision: centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient engineers through
sustainable practices. Mission M1: Impart fundamental knowledge through innovative teaching. M2: Inculcate critical thinking, ethics,
lifelong learning. M3: Cultivate all-round competencies for career and higher education.
